headlineSummary: Anthropic and OpenAI were both viewed positively, with a close tie; Anthropic had medium evidence [E1][E2][E5] while OpenAI’s positive signal rested on limited evidence [E3][E4].

ranking.summary: Anthropic drew mostly positive feedback for coding and long-context use, though one comment mixed praise with criticism about maintainability and style [E1][E2][E5].
OpenAI was praised for strong proof-related help and an easy Codex experience, but the signal is based on limited evidence [E3][E4].

evidence.excerpt: For coding, Claude is amazing - mainly because of its curated skills

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly praises Claude's coding performance, using the strong evaluative word 'amazing'.

evidence.excerpt: This has not been my experience with Opus since Anthropic released the 1M token context window for use under the subscription plans. I routinely push past 500k tokens, even sometimes up to around 800k tokens, and don't see this problem.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Opus does not show the reported long-context failure in their usage, which is mild positive evidence about Anthropic's model quality.

evidence.excerpt: ChatGPT can often prove something in minutes and 10 - 100 iterations that would take me, a human who has limited but non-zero proof assistant experience but significant domain experience in the lemmas being proven, much much longer.

annotations.rationale: This is strong explicit praise for ChatGPT's effectiveness on proof tasks, saying it can do in minutes what would take the commenter much longer.

evidence.excerpt: I've had Codex Lean-ify a couple of my hobby proofs, it was extremely easy.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly reports an easy, successful experience with Codex on proof-related work.

evidence.excerpt: I guess Python is an ok target for agentic coding, but my god do look Claude's commit messages pretentious, with code bases quickly heading into absolute unmaintainability. At least it had found gross JS injection vectors in a Django app that really shouldn't have made it through a code review, architecture level as they were, but oh well.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt mixes criticism of Claude's outputs as 'pretentious' and unmaintainable with a specific positive note that it found serious JS injection issues.
